extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first third of the series (roughly indices 0–7), values start moderately high (around 0.2–0.3) and then decrease toward near-zero.  
R2: Around the middle (roughly indices 8–13), there is a steep positive rise from near-zero up to a high value around 0.6–0.9.  
R3: After this middle peak, values stay relatively high (plateau) for a few steps with only small oscillations (no sharp drop).  
R4: In the final third (roughly indices 18–24), values decrease gradually from the plateau, but remain clearly above zero (around 0.2–0.6) at the end.  
R5: The global maximum occurs in the late middle or early last third (approximately indices 12–20), not right at the very end.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first third of the series (roughly indices 0–7), values start low (around 0–0.1) and remain low with only slight variations.  
R2: From the early-middle region (roughly indices 7–12), there is a steep positive rise from these very low values to a high value around 0.6–0.8.  
R3: After reaching this high level, the series forms a short plateau or gentle hump without large oscillations.  
R4: In the final third (roughly indices 18–24), values show a sharp, almost monotonic drop from the high region down close to zero (around 0–0.2) by the end.  
R5: The global maximum is followed quickly by this strong downward trend so that the last few points are among the lowest in the series.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts at a moderate value (around 0.2–0.3) and decreases during the first third of the time, approaching but not necessarily reaching zero.  
R2: In the middle third (roughly indices 8–14), the series rises very steeply from near-zero to a relatively high peak (around 0.6–0.9).  
R3: After the first main peak, the series stays at a relatively high level with one or more smaller local peaks, without an immediate sharp drop.  
R4: In the final third, the values decrease gradually from the high plateau, but do not fall all the way close to zero by the end (end value still around 0.15–0.5).  
R5: Overall shape: early decline → steep middle rise → broad high plateau with mild undulations → moderate final decline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a low value (near 0.0–0.1) and stays low or slightly decreases in the first third of the time.  
R2: In the middle third (roughly indices 8–14), the series rises steeply from low values to a high peak (around 0.6–0.8), similar in steepness to class 0.  
R3: Immediately after the peak, the series shows only a short high plateau (or a single main peak) with limited secondary oscillations.  
R4: In the final third, the values drop sharply and monotonically from the high level down to very low values (end value close to 0.0–0.15).  
R5: Overall shape: low and flat start → steep middle rise → brief peak/plateau → strong, almost linear fall to near-zero at the end.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first third of the series, values start around 0.2–0.3 and decrease toward (or slightly above) 0.0.  
R2: Around the middle (roughly indices 8–13), the series rises sharply from near 0.0 to a peak between about 0.6 and 0.9.  
R3: After the sharp rise, there is a short plateau or small oscillations near the peak (values stay roughly between 0.5 and 0.9 for several time steps).  
R4: In the last third, the series decreases gradually from the peak region but remains mostly above 0.2 until close to the end.  
R5: The final value is clearly above 0.1 (i.e., the series does not drop close to zero at the end).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series begins very close to 0.0 (initial values below about 0.1) and stays near 0.0 in the first few time steps.  
R2: Around indices 6–12, the series rises sharply from near 0.0 to a peak between about 0.6 and 0.9.  
R3: After the sharp rise, there is a broad high region where values remain relatively high (around 0.5–0.8) for several time steps.  
R4: In the last third, the series decreases steeply from the high region down toward low values.  
R5: The final value is close to 0.0 (below about 0.15), i.e., the series ends near the baseline.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts at a relatively higher level (around 0.2–0.35) and then decreases during the first few time steps.  
R2: It stays very low (close to 0) roughly between time 4 and 8.  
R3: A steep increase occurs starting around time 8–10, reaching a clear peak between time 12 and 16.  
R4: After the main peak, the series remains in a moderately high band (about 0.5–0.9) for several time steps instead of dropping immediately.  
R5: The final decline from the last high region is gradual, not a sharp fall all the way to near-zero by the end.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a very low level (near 0) and remains low for an extended initial segment (up to about time 6–7).  
R2: A gradual rise begins from this low region, leading into a very sharp increase around time 9–11.  
R3: A dominant single peak appears between time 12 and 18, with values around 0.6–0.85.  
R4: After the peak, there is a long, steep decline so that values near the final time steps are close to 0.1 or lower.  
R5: Overall, the shape is “low for long → sharp rise → one main peak → long sharp fall,” with no extended high plateau after the peak.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first third of the series, values start around 0.2–0.3 and clearly decrease to near 0.0.  
R2: Around the middle (roughly indices 8–13), there is a sharp rise from near 0.0 to a peak between 0.6 and 0.9.  
R3: After this main peak, values stay relatively high (plateau) for several points, fluctuating mildly but remaining above about 0.5.  
R4: The peak region and subsequent plateau are relatively broad in time (spread over many indices).  
R5: In the last quarter, values gradually decline from the high plateau down to around 0.2–0.5, but do not fall sharply to near zero.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first third of the series, values remain very low (near 0.0–0.1) with only a slight downward or flat trend.  
R2: Around the middle (roughly indices 8–13), there is a sharp, almost monotonic rise from near 0.0 to a peak between 0.6 and 0.8.  
R3: After this peak, values remain high only for a short interval; the high region is narrower than in Class 0.  
R4: In the last third, values drop steeply from the high region down to near 0.0–0.2.  
R5: Overall, the shape is “low–sharp rise–brief high–steep fall,” with a more pronounced, rapid final decline than in Class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first third of the series (roughly indices 0–7), the values start moderately high (around 0.2–0.3) and then decrease toward near-zero (small downward slope).
R2: Around the middle of the series (roughly indices 8–13), there is a sharp increase from near-zero up to a clear peak.
R3: The global maximum occurs in the middle third of the series, not in the last third.
R4: After the middle peak, values remain relatively high for a short plateau or gentle undulation rather than dropping immediately.
R5: In the last third (roughly indices 16–23), values gradually decrease from medium/high back down to low/moderate, without an extremely steep final drop.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first third of the series (roughly indices 0–7), the values start very low and stay close to zero with only a slight decline or flat shape.
R2: From early-middle to middle (roughly indices 7–13), there is a sharp rise from near-zero to high values.
R3: After this rise, the series forms a broad high plateau or gently varying high region, with no pronounced secondary increases.
R4: The global maximum occurs just after the sharp rise, toward the start of the last third of the series.
R5: In the last third (roughly indices 16–23), there is a strong, steep monotonic-like decline from high values down toward near-zero.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: Early segment (time 0–5) starts above 0.15 and decreases toward a small minimum around 0.02–0.05.  
R2: Middle rise (time 8–13) begins near 0.1–0.2 and increases steeply to a peak between 0.6 and 0.9.  
R3: Around the peak (time 13–18) the series stays relatively high (mostly above 0.5) with small up‑and‑down fluctuations.  
R4: Late segment (time 18–24) shows a clear decline from the high plateau, ending around 0.15–0.5.  
R5: Overall, the first value is clearly higher than the last local minimum before the big rise (distinct initial drop then strong climb).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: Early segment (time 0–5) stays very low and flat, mostly between 0 and 0.05, with only a gentle decline.  
R2: The main rise (time 8–13) starts from near-zero values and shoots up steeply to a peak between 0.6 and 0.8.  
R3: Around the peak (time 13–18) the series either slightly decreases or forms a broad, smooth high plateau (values roughly 0.5–0.8) without pronounced oscillations.  
R4: Late segment (time 18–24) shows a long, strong monotonic or near‑monotonic decline from the peak down to near 0.1–0.35.  
R5: Overall, the first part of the series remains closer to zero than in Class 0 (flatter and lower pre‑rise baseline).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first third of the series (early time), values start around 0.2–0.35 and decrease toward near 0, forming an early dip.
R2: Around the middle of the series, values rise sharply from near 0 to a high peak in the range 0.6–0.9.
R3: After the main peak, values remain relatively high and flat for some time (a plateau) before declining.
R4: In the last third of the series, values decrease but typically stay above about 0.15–0.2 (they do not fall close to 0).
R5: The minimum value of the whole series occurs in the early segment, not at the end.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first third of the series, values stay very low and flat (near 0, below about 0.1) before any rise.
R2: Around the middle of the series, values rise sharply from near 0 to a high peak in the range 0.6–0.85.
R3: After the main peak, there is no long high plateau; the series begins to drop relatively soon after peaking.
R4: In the last third of the series, values decrease strongly and approach very low levels (down to about 0.1 or below).
R5: The minimum value of the whole series occurs near the end or is comparable at start and end (the ending segment is as low or lower than the early segment).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first third of the series (early time), values start around 0.2–0.3 and decrease slightly toward near 0.0.  
R2: The minimum value occurs early (around index 4–6).  
R3: The steep main rise begins later, around index 8–10, going from near 0.1 to a peak around 0.6–0.9.  
R4: After the peak, values form a broad plateau with small oscillations, staying relatively high for several time steps.  
R5: The final decline is moderate: values decrease but typically remain around 0.2–0.5 at the end, not near zero.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first third of the series, values start very low (near 0.0–0.1) and stay low and flat for several steps.  
R2: The minimum also occurs early, but the series remains close to this minimum for longer before rising.  
R3: The steep main rise begins slightly later than in Class 0 (around index 10–12), going quickly to a peak around 0.6–0.8.  
R4: After the peak, values stay high for a short plateau and then drop sharply rather than gradually.  
R5: The final values approach near-zero again (around 0.0–0.15), showing a strong end-of-series decline.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first 3–4 time steps the series starts at a moderate value (around 0.2–0.3) and decreases toward near 0.  
R2: From roughly time 4–10 the series stays close to 0 (flat low “valley”).  
R3: Around time 10–13 the series rises very sharply from near 0 to a high peak.  
R4: After the peak, between about time 14–18, the series remains on a high plateau with only small oscillations.  
R5: From about time 18 to the end the series decreases gradually from that high level but stays above the starting level.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first 5–6 time steps the series stays very close to 0 (lower than class 0’s start).  
R2: From about time 6–12 the series rises from near 0 to its main peak, but the rise is more gradual/longer than in class 0.  
R3: After the peak, between roughly time 13–18, the series stays at a high plateau or gently varying high level.  
R4: From about time 18 to the end the series falls sharply and continuously from the high level down close to 0.  
R5: The ending value is substantially lower than the starting value (strong late drop).
